REPLY MEMORANDUM OF DALY CITY HB IN SUPPORT OF GENERAL AND SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB, INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD FACTS REGARDING HOW DEFENDANT BREACHED THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD FACTS REGARDING DEFENDANT'S DUTY TO DISCLOSE THE FACT THAT IT WOULD FILE A 1099-MISC TAX FORM. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
APPENDIX OF FEDERAL TAX AUTHORITIES FILED BY DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E).) AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION, THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT ("TAC") ALLEGES NO SPECIFIC FACTS AS TO HOW DEFENDANT BREACHED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT OR HOW ANY ALLEGED BREACH CAUSED DAMAGE TO PLAINTIFF. AS TO THE "EXEMPLARY DAMAGES" CAUSE OF ACTION, THE TAC APPEARS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT. THE TAC DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT CONCEALED ANYTHING. THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT B TO THE TAC EXPRESSLY STATES THAT THE SETTLEMENT WOULD BE REPORTED AS NONEMPLOYEE COMPENSATION. (PAR. 3(B).) THE FORM 1099-MISC ATTACHED TO THE TAC AS EXHIBIT C SHOWS THAT THE COMPENSATION WAS REPORTED AS NONEMPLOYEE COMPENSATION, IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN LIGHT OF THE FACT PLAINTIFF HAS HAD FOUR OPPORTUNITIES TO PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING A CAUSE OF ACTION AND HAS BEEN UNABLE TO DO SO. (AFTER HEARING, THE COURT SIGNED THE ORDER THAT WAS SENT BY EMAIL.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB INC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE 510, THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE 226.7, FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE 226, FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 17203, AND SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. EACH OF PLAINTIFF'S ABOVEMENTIONED CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY THE GENERAL RELEASE PROVISION OF THE SIGNED SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. (THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, EX. B.) MOREOVER, "NONEMPLOYEE COMPENSATION" IS NOT AMBIGUOUS. EVEN IF IT WERE AMBIGUOUS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED ANY PHYSICAL INJURY OR SICKNESS ARISING FROM HIS EMPLOYMENT WITH DEFENDANT TO MAKE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS PAYMENTS NONTAXABLE. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED SPECIFIC FACTS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT MISREPRESENTED ANYTHING TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD OR DECLARATORY RELIEF. THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS HAD FOUR OPPORTUNITIES TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS BUT HAS FAILED TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Continued To Jun-04-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-14-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB INC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted as to Plaintiff's second cause of action for violation of Labor Code 510, third cause of action for violation of Labor Code 226.7, fourth cause of action for violation of Labor Code 226, fifth cause of action for violation of Business & Professions Code 17203, and sixth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Each of Plaintiff's abovementioned causes of action are barred by the general release provision of the signed settlement agreement. (Third Amended Complaint, Ex. B.) Moreover, "nonemployee compensation" is not ambiguous. Even if it were ambiguous, Plaintiff has not alleged any physical injury or sickness arising from his employment with Defendant to make emotional distress payments nontaxable. Plaintiff has not alleged specific facts to show that Defendant misrepresented anything to state a cause of action for fraud or declaratory relief. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend because Plaintiff has had four opportunities to allege sufficient facts but has failed to do so. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-28-2014 Continued To Apr-30-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) As to the breach of contract cause of action, the Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") alleges no specific facts as to how Defendant breached the Settlement Agreement or how any alleged breach caused damage to Plaintiff. As to the "exemplary damages" cause of action, the TAC appears to allege a cause of action for fraudulent concealment. The TAC does not allege facts establishing that Defendant concealed anything. The Settlement Agreement attached as Exhibit B to the TAC expressly states that the settlement would be reported as nonemployee compensation. (Par. 3(b).) The Form 1099-MISC attached to the TAC as Exhibit C shows that the compensation was reported as nonemployee compensation, in accordance with the terms of the Settlement Agreement. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact Plaintiff has had four opportunities to plead facts establishing a cause of action and has been unable to do so. (After hearing, the Court signed the order that was sent by email.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied as moot in light of the ruling on demurrer. (After hearing, the Court signed the order that was sent by email.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2014 Continued To Mar-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-27-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB, INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. As to the first cause of action, Defendant's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to plead facts regarding how Defendant breached the terms of the Settlement Agreement. As to the seventh cause of action, Defendant's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend, if he can do in good faith, to plead facts regarding Defendant's duty to disclose the fact that it would file a 1099-Misc tax form. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
